---
title: "Angela Larson"
image: images/front.image.png
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 20em
  links: 
    - text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/angelalarson
      icon: "github"
      target: _blank
---

## About Me!

I am a first year PhD student at Tulane University in the Earth and Environmental Sciences Department, advised by Dr. Thomas DeCarlo. Broadly, I study coral reef microclimates and coral bleaching.

I completed my undergraduate degree at UC Santa Barbara majoring in Earth Science with an emphasis in Climate and Environment. I became highly involved in research early at UC Santa Barbara as a Field-based Undergraduate Engagement through Research, Teaching, and Education (FUERTE) fellow. Beyond my academics, I served as the president of VocalMotion, UCSB Women's A Cappella group, for two years and played on the UCSB Women's Rugby team for four years.

## Education

Tulane University - Earth and Environmental Sciences PhD as a Louisiana Board of Regents/SREB Doctoral Fellow

University of California, Santa Barbara – Earth Science, emphasis in Climate and Environment, B.S. - 2025
